skill.name: lark-doc
skill.description: Create, search, read, update, upload, and download Lark cloud documents through lark-cli workflows.
skill.summary: Manage Lark documents from an AI coding assistant with guided lark-cli commands.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, entropy, and path traversal patterns. Review found no malicious payload or prompt injection, but confirmed that the skill intentionally runs networked lark-cli workflows that can read, create, update, delete, upload, download, and change permissions on Lark documents. Publish with clear warnings about confirmation, credentials, document scope, and local file paths.

medium_findings.title: Networked Lark CLI Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to use lark-cli shortcuts for Lark document search, creation, fetching, updating, media insertion, media download, and whiteboard update. These actions use authenticated Lark APIs and can expose or modify workspace content if used with broad credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines define networked lark-cli workflows and the search API context. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates real data access risk.
medium_findings.remediation: Require scoped Lark credentials, confirm user intent before API calls, and avoid returning unnecessary document content.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Document Update Modes
medium_findings.description: The update shortcut supports delete_range, replace_all, and overwrite modes. The documentation warns that overwrite can clear a document and may lose images, comments, and other content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly describes deletion and overwrite operations and acknowledges data loss risk. This is not malicious, but misuse can damage documents.
medium_findings.remediation: Ask for explicit confirmation before destructive modes and prefer targeted replacements or append operations.

medium_findings.title: Local File Upload and Download Paths
medium_findings.description: The media shortcuts upload local files into Lark documents and download remote media to local output paths. Incorrect file selection or output paths could disclose local files or overwrite user files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines show explicit --file and --output path handling for upload and download workflows. The risk depends on user-selected paths and credential scope.
medium_findings.remediation: Confirm local paths before upload or download, avoid sensitive directories, and refuse ambiguous file paths.

medium_findings.title: Automatic Permission Grant Workflow
medium_findings.description: The create workflow instructs the agent to grant the current user full_access when a document is created by a bot identity. This is useful, but it changes document permissions and could affect the wrong account if identity resolution is wrong.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly describe granting full_access after bot-created documents. The workflow is intentional, but permission changes require careful identity checks.
medium_findings.remediation: Confirm the target identity before granting full_access and report failed or skipped authorization clearly.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby and shell execution alerts are false positives caused by fenced command examples and inline backticks in Markdown. They are not embedded executable scripts in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown command examples, not Ruby backtick execution. The skill still intentionally asks agents to run lark-cli, which is covered in medium findings.
low_findings.remediation: No code removal is needed. Marketplace warnings should describe the intended CLI execution model.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal and Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Documentation Artifacts
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts map to relative documentation links such as shared skill references. The hardcoded URLs are Lark URL examples and placeholders used to explain token handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited paths and URLs are documentation references and examples. No file read outside the skill package or network call to an unknown domain is implemented in these Markdown files.
low_findings.remediation: Keep relative references clear and avoid using real tenant URLs in examples.

low_findings.title: Entropy and Weak Crypto Alerts Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The high entropy and weak cryptography findings appear to come from dense Chinese text, Markdown tables, and repeated tokens. No encoded payload, cryptographic function, or hidden binary content was found in reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner reported entropy and weak crypto patterns, but manual review did not find obfuscated payloads or crypto usage. Confidence is lower because only semantic review was performed.
low_findings.remediation: No remediation required unless future versions add encoded data or executable assets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Overwrite and Delete Update Modes
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents update modes that can delete sections or overwrite an entire Lark document. These commands should require explicit user confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and warnings directly describe destructive update modes. Risk is clear but bounded to authorized Lark documents.
dangerous_patterns.remediation: Use append or small replace operations by default and confirm destructive edits.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Upload Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The media insert shortcut accepts a local path and uploads that file into a Lark document. Sensitive files could be exposed if the path is wrong.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The --file path is a documented required input. The behavior is intended, but it creates local file disclosure risk.
dangerous_patterns.remediation: Confirm file name, path, and document target before upload.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Download Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The media download shortcut writes Lark media to a local output path. It should not write into sensitive directories or overwrite important files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The --output path is documented as required and may auto-add an extension. The risk is path misuse rather than malicious code.
dangerous_patterns.remediation: Use a user-approved output directory and avoid overwriting existing files.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable ways to work with Lark documents from an AI assistant. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through lark-cli workflows for search, creation, updates, and media handling.
actual_capabilities: Search Lark docs, Wiki pages, and spreadsheet files by keyword.
Create Lark documents from Lark-flavored Markdown.
Fetch document content as Markdown or readable output.
Update documents by appending, replacing, inserting, deleting, or overwriting content.
Upload local images or files into documents.
Download document media and whiteboard thumbnails to local paths.
limitations: Requires lark-cli and valid Lark authentication.
Cannot directly edit existing whiteboard content through lark-doc.
Wiki URLs may need token resolution before document operations.
Overwrite mode can lose rich content that cannot be rebuilt from Markdown.

use_cases.title: Create Team Documents
use_cases.description: Turn meeting notes, plans, or briefs into structured Lark documents with headings, tables, and callouts.

use_cases.title: Find Workspace Resources
use_cases.description: Search Lark cloud space for documents, Wiki pages, and spreadsheets before choosing the right workflow.

use_cases.title: Maintain Living Documentation
use_cases.description: Append release notes, replace sections, or insert updates into existing Lark documents with targeted commands.

prompt_templates.title: Search for a Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Lark for documents about "quarterly planning" and show the most relevant titles, links, and file types.

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Lark document titled "Project Kickoff" from these notes. Use clear headings and a simple action table.

prompt_templates.title: Update a Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the "Risks" section in this Lark document with the new risk list. Avoid changing any other section.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Media and Permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: Insert this local diagram into the Lark document, confirm the target file path, then verify the document remains accessible to me.

output_examples.input: Search for the onboarding handbook in Lark.
output_examples.output: Returns matching Lark documents with titles, links, document types, and pagination details when more results exist.

output_examples.input: Create a launch plan from my notes.
output_examples.output: Creates a Lark document with a title, organized sections, action items, and a direct document link.

output_examples.input: Add this architecture image to the design document.
output_examples.output: Confirms the local file path, uploads the image, inserts it at the document end, and reports the media token.

best_practices: Confirm document targets before write, delete, upload, or permission actions.
Prefer append or small replacements over full overwrite operations.
Use scoped Lark credentials and avoid unnecessary document content exposure.
anti_patterns: Do not overwrite a document unless the user explicitly approves the data loss risk.
Do not upload local files from ambiguous or sensitive paths.
Do not assume Wiki URLs are direct document tokens.

faq.question: Does this skill require Lark authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires lark-cli and credentials with the needed Lark scopes.

faq.question: Can it search spreadsheets too?
faq.answer: Yes. The search workflow can find documents, Wiki pages, and spreadsheet files.

faq.question: Can it edit existing whiteboards?
faq.answer: No. It can create blank whiteboards, but existing whiteboard editing needs the whiteboard skill.

faq.question: Can it download images from a document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can extract media tokens and download images, files, or whiteboard thumbnails.

faq.question: Is overwrite mode safe?
faq.answer: Overwrite mode is risky because it can remove rich content, comments, and media references.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
